title: erucastrum varium var. campestre
intro: Erucastrum varium var. campestre, also known as Common Hoary Cress, is a biennial or short-lived perennial plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) with a distinctive white or yellowish flower. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high content of glucosinolates. The health benefits of Erucastrum varium var. campestre include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
